findings.description: reachable() BFS never checks `seen` before enqueueing a neighbor, so on the explicitly-promised cyclic graph the queue grows without bound (non-terminating / OOM), and even on a DAG diamond fan-in causes exponential re-expansion of nodes.

count_paths() recurses with no memoization, yielding O(2^n) blowup on diamond-shaped DAGs (every shared subpath is recomputed from scratch); add an lru_cache/visited-memo on (node,dst) to make it O(V+E).

self._edges is a defaultdict(list), so every read of a leaf/unknown node's neighbors here (and on lines 40, 61) silently inserts a new empty list, causing steady memory growth on read-only traversals and turning these 'reads' into non-thread-safe mutations; use self._edges.get(node, ()).

count_paths() has unbounded recursion depth (RecursionError on long chains) and infinite-recurses on any cycle despite the 'DAG assumed' comment — adjacent correctness issue; defer detailed handling to critic-logic.

reachable() marks a node 'seen' at enqueue time but never adds `start` itself, and combined with the missing visited-guard the returned set is also semantically wrong (excludes start, may duplicate-process) — defer detail to critic-logic.
